results.name: BiPC

results.question: In "Physics (2nd Year)", which statement is most accurate about cellular process?

Genetic Inheritance is valid only if core conditions are ignored.
Organic Reaction Logic is a memory cue, not a defensible explanation.

results.name: BiPC
results.subject: BiPC
results.question: A learner applies physiological regulation in a new problem from "Physics (2nd Year)". What is the best first step?
results.correct_answer: Define conditions, apply physiological regulation stepwise, and verify the conclusion with evidence.
incorrect_answers: Start from Physics (2nd Year) inference and skip checking constraints.
Use biochemical pathway as a shortcut and avoid interpretation.
Pick cellular process by intuition and finalise without verification.

results.name: BiPC
results.subject: BiPC
results.question: Which option shows a valid example of genetic inheritance in "Physics (2nd Year)"?

An example where Physics (2nd Year) inference is stated without evidence.

results.name: BiPC
results.subject: BiPC
results.question: Choose the most defensible correction for this claim in "Physics (2nd Year)": "ecological balance works without constraints."

incorrect_answers: Physics (2Nd Year) removes the need for all constraints in every case.

Cellular Process is enough even when data contradict the claim.
